name: Navahermosa and San Martín de Montalbán Loop
name_original: Navahermosa y San Martín de Montalbán – circular desdeSanta Ana de Pusa
description: A difficult 51.1-mile road cycling route from Santa Ana de Pusa, climbing 2986 feet through the Montes de Toledo.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santa Ana De Pusa traverse the varied topography of the Pusa River valley and the foothills of the Montes de Toledo. The region features a mix of rolling hills and more challenging climbs, with routes utilizing secondary regional roads for a peaceful experience. Extensive holm oak forests and dehesas characterize the natural environment, providing scenic backdrops for rides. The area offers diverse terrain suitable for various fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Santa Ana De Pusa The most popular road cycling route is Navahermosa and San Martín de Montalbán Loop, a difficult 51.1 miles (82.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 49 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Montes de Toledo and Cabañeros Park Loop, a difficult 72.5 miles (116.7 km) path. This route offers substantial climbing through the natural beauty of the Montes de Toledo. Local road cyclists also love the Montes de Toledo – circular to the sierras of Castillazo and Horno from Los Navalucillos, a 55.4 miles (89.2 km) trail leading through the sierras, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Santa Ana De Pusa is defined by the Pusa River valley, holm oak forests, and the challenging climbs of the Montes de Toledo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier routes to demanding ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 140 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Ana De Pusa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: The region offers a diverse range of terrain, from rolling hills in the Pusa River valley to more challenging climbs as you approach the Montes de Toledo. You'll cycle through extensive holm oak forests and dehesas, providing a scenic and tranquil backdrop away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: Yes, the area is known for its demanding routes, especially those venturing into the Montes de Toledo. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025236984/montes-de-toledo-and-cabaneros-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo and Cabañeros Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 116.7 km path with significant elevation gain, offering substantial climbing through natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling options for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride?
A: While many routes in the area are challenging, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and cover shorter distances, making them suitable for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes near Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: You'll find routes traversing the picturesque Pusa River valley, with opportunities to see features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabonero Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the Estrechura del río Pusa (Narrows of the Pusa River). The landscape is also characterized by beautiful holm oak forests and dehesas, especially as you approach the Montes de Toledo.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You might pass through villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8114787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Navalmorales&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8114657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Pusa&lt;/a&gt;, which feature historical buildings. Santa Ana de Pusa itself has its Town Hall and Church. You can also find archaeological findings, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visigothic Stone Sarcophagus of Los Navalucillos&lt;/a&gt;, highlighting the area's past.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: Santa Ana de Pusa is an excellent destination for outdoor activities due to its varied topography and peaceful routes. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029383043/navahermosa-and-san-martin-de-montalban-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navahermosa and San Martín de Montalbán Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 82.2 km trail.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the peaceful experience offered by the secondary regional roads, the varied and scenic landscapes, and the challenging climbs in the Montes de Toledo. The extensive holm oak forests and the Pusa River valley provide a beautiful backdrop for rides, making it a popular choice for those seeking tranquility and diverse terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Montes de Toledo?
A: Absolutely. The proximity to the Montes de Toledo provides opportunities for more demanding rides with significant elevation gains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025254735/montes-de-toledo-circular-to-the-sierras-of-castillazo-and-horno-from-los-navalucillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo – circular to the sierras of Castillazo and Horno from Los Navalucillos&lt;/a&gt; lead through these impressive sierras.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Pusa River?
A: Yes, several routes traverse the Pusa River valley, offering picturesque views and a more tranquil cycling experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Navalmorales – Estrechura of the Pusa River loop from Los Navalucillos&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that explores the river's surroundings.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: There are several road cycling routes available around Santa Ana de Pusa, with a total of 4 routes currently listed. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including easy and difficult options.

Q: Are there any routes that connect different villages in the area?
A: Yes, some routes connect the charming villages in the region. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Navalmorales – Los Navalucillos loop from Los Navalmorales&lt;/a&gt; allows you to explore multiple settlements and their surrounding landscapes.
tips:
Navahermosa is a highly valued destination for those seeking nature, tranquility, and historical heritage. Located in the Montes de Toledo mountains, surrounded by hills and streams, it's ideal for hiking, cultural routes, and rural getaways.
Church of San Miguel Arcángel
A 16th-century church with Renaissance elements and a 19th-century tower. Its Mudéjar coffered ceiling and altarpieces are particularly noteworthy.

Although the town's biggest attractions, like its castle and Roman bridge, are all on the outskirts and not reachable by road bike, the town itself has a pretty church dedicated to Saint Andrew and a couple of good bars. for a stop

Despite having only about 350 inhabitants, Santa Ana de Pusa undoubtedly stands out for its numerous and original popular festivals. Beginning on January 20 with the Fiesta de los Perros, the celebrations continue at Easter with the Burning of Judas and have their maximum expression in their patron saint festivities dedicated to Santa Ana in the month of July. There is also a festival called El Cristo in mid-September.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is quite demanding, covering over 82 kilometers. It typically takes around 3 hours and 49 minutes to complete on a racebike, depending on your pace and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. It involves significant climbs and descents, with an elevation gain of over 900 meters. Cyclists should have very good fitness and be prepared for a challenging ride through the Montes de Toledo.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows well-paved roads, making it suitable for racebikes. You'll encounter varied terrain typical of the Montes de Toledo, including rolling hills, significant ascents, and descents, all within a scenic natural environment.

faqs.question: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll pass through charming villages and experience the natural beauty of the region. Highlights include the Church of San Miguel Arcángel in Navahermosa and the Church of San Andrés in San Martín de Montalbán. The starting point in Santa Ana de Pusa also features its distinctive Town Hall and Church.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to road cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for road cycling in this region. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for a more comfortable and enjoyable ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route in Santa Ana de Pusa?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Santa Ana de Pusa is a small town. You should be able to find suitable street parking near the town center or the Town Hall and Church, which serves as a good starting point for the circular route.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it's generally not suitable for dogs to accompany you on the bike itself. If you're looking for dog-friendly activities in the wider Montes de Toledo area, you might consider walking trails, but this specific route is designed for road cycling.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads through the towns and countryside.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly overlap with other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Camino de los Montes de Toledo - Etapa 4, Camino de Santa Ana de Pusa a Los Navalmorales, and the Camino Real de Guadalupe por los Montes de Toledo, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for refreshments or supplies along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Navahermosa and San Martín de Montalbán, as well as starting in Santa Ana de Pusa. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, shops, or other services where you can refuel and rest during your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging road cycling trip like this?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling gear. Sun protection is also advisable, especially during warmer months. A fully charged phone and a navigation device are highly recommended.
